title: Interactive Association Connector Builder
objective: Add a drag-to-connect interaction to the Association Graph (Devices view) so users can create and delete Z-Wave associations directly on the canvas without touching the side panel. A 'Connect Mode' toggle enables vis-network's manipulation API; dragging from one node to another opens the existing DialogAssociation pre-filled with both endpoints locked; clicking an existing edge in connect mode shows a deletion snackbar.

in: Connect Mode toggle button in GroupGraph toolbar (Devices view only)
vis-network manipulation.addEdge wiring for drag-to-connect ghost edge
onConnectDrop handler: guard logic + open DialogAssociation with lockedSource/lockedTarget
lockedSource / lockedTarget props on DialogAssociation to pre-fill and disable fields
_assocData stored on every visEdge in paintAssociationsView for reverse-lookup
selectEdge handler: show delete confirmation snackbar with 5-second window
confirmDeleteEdge: call removeAssociations, repaint graph, sync Pinia store
Auto-exit connect mode when user switches view mode
out: Multi-endpoint source drag (covered by side panel)
Connect mode in Groups / Node-to-Groups / Shared views
Changes to existing side-panel add/remove flow

functional_requirements.title: visEdge metadata
functional_requirements.description: Every edge added to the vis DataSet in paintAssociationsView must carry _assocData containing sourceNodeId, sourceEndpoint, groupId, targetNodeId, targetEndpoint so the delete handler can identify the exact association without a reverse-lookup.
acceptance_criteria: Every edge in Devices view has _assocData set
_assocData.sourceNodeId matches the node the group belongs to
_assocData.targetNodeId matches the member node

functional_requirements.title: DialogAssociation locked props
functional_requirements.description: DialogAssociation.vue gains optional lockedSource and lockedTarget props (both Object|null). When set, the corresponding fields are pre-filled from the prop value and rendered disabled. Source endpoint defaults to 0 and is hidden when source is locked. Validation runs normally. Emitted payload is unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: Passing lockedSource pre-fills and disables the source node field
Passing lockedTarget pre-fills and disables the target node combobox
Association validation fires on dialog open when props are locked
Normal (unlocked) dialog usage is unaffected

functional_requirements.title: Connect Mode toggle
functional_requirements.description: A toolbar button with icon add_link toggles connectMode (boolean, default false). Button is disabled with tooltip when viewMode !== 'associations'. A watcher sets connectMode = false when viewMode changes. An info banner is shown while connect mode is active.
acceptance_criteria: Toggle button appears in GroupGraph toolbar
Button is disabled and shows tooltip outside Devices view
Switching view mode while connect mode is active exits connect mode
Info hint banner is visible while connect mode is on and dismissible

functional_requirements.title: vis-network manipulation wiring
functional_requirements.description: Watch connectMode. On true: call network.setOptions({ manipulation: { enabled: true, initiallyActive: true, addEdge: callback } }) where callback calls onConnectDrop and passes null to the vis callback to suppress vis adding a real edge. On false: call network.setOptions({ manipulation: { enabled: false } }) and clear pendingDeleteEdge.
acceptance_criteria: Entering connect mode enables vis manipulation with addEdge callback
Dragging from a node shows vis-network ghost edge and port handles
Exiting connect mode disables manipulation cleanly
Re-painting the graph does not re-enable manipulation unexpectedly

functional_requirements.title: onConnectDrop handler
functional_requirements.description: onConnectDrop(fromVisId, toVisId): strip node_ prefix to get integer nodeIds; guard same-node and group-type vis nodes; look up ZUINode objects from Pinia store; set connectorSource and connectorTarget data properties; set showConnectorDialog = true. DialogAssociation instance in template wired to these properties with lockedSource and lockedTarget. On dialog add event: call addMember(). On cancel: no-op.
acceptance_criteria: Dropping on same node does nothing
Dropping on a group diamond does nothing
Dropping on a valid target node opens DialogAssociation pre-filled and locked
Confirming the dialog creates the association and repaints the graph with Pinia store updated
Cancelling the dialog leaves no new association

functional_requirements.title: Edge-click delete with snackbar
functional_requirements.description: Inside createNetwork, add selectEdge handler active only when connectMode is true. Gets _assocData from the clicked edge; sets pendingDeleteEdge and showDeleteSnackbar = true. Snackbar shows source and target node names, Confirm and Cancel buttons, 5-second timeout. confirmDeleteEdge() calls removeAssociations via apiRequest, fetches fresh associations, updates associationsMap, calls setAssociations, calls paintGraph. deselectEdge handler and Cancel button clear pendingDeleteEdge.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking an edge in connect mode shows delete snackbar with node names
Confirming removes the association and repaints the graph
Cancelling or snackbar timeout deselects edge with no change
Clicking an edge outside connect mode does not show snackbar

non_functional_requirements.requirement: All existing side-panel add/remove flows must continue working exactly as before
non_functional_requirements.measure: No regressions in existing AssociationGroups or GroupGraph side-panel interactions

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Connect mode toggle and ghost-edge rendering must not cause frame drops in the vis-network canvas
non_functional_requirements.measure: No visible jank during drag on a 34-node network

non_functional_requirements.requirement: All new UI elements (button, banner, snackbar) must use Vuetify 3 components consistent with the existing codebase style
non_functional_requirements.measure: No raw HTML buttons or custom CSS; all colours from Vuetify theme

key_dependencies: vis-network manipulation API (built-in, no new package)
Pinia store: nodes, nodesMap, associationsMap, setAssociations
technical_architecture.data_flow: User drag → vis manipulation.addEdge callback → onConnectDrop → DialogAssociation (locked) → addMember() → apiRequest(addAssociations) → getAssociations refresh → setAssociations(Pinia) → paintGraph()

risks.risk: vis-network manipulation toolbar overlaps existing UI chrome
risks.mitigation: Set manipulation.controlNodeStyle and use initiallyActive:true to skip the vis default toolbar; only the ghost-edge behaviour is needed, not vis's own add/delete buttons

risks.risk: paintGraph() called mid-drag recreates the Network instance and loses manipulation mode
risks.mitigation: In createNetwork(), re-apply manipulation options based on current connectMode value; watcher on connectMode re-calls enableConnectMode after any repaint

risks.risk: DialogAssociation locked-prop changes break existing callers that pass no props
risks.mitigation: Both props default to null; all conditional rendering uses v-if on prop presence; existing usages pass no props and hit none of the new code paths

tasks.title: Store _assocData on each visEdge in paintAssociationsView
Add lockedSource / lockedTarget props to DialogAssociation
Add Connect Mode toggle button to GroupGraph toolbar
Wire vis-network manipulation API for drag-to-connect
Implement onConnectDrop — open DialogAssociation from drag result
Implement edge-click delete with confirmation snackbar

acceptance_criteria: Connect toggle button visible in toolbar, disabled outside Devices view
Dragging from node A to node B opens DialogAssociation pre-filled with both nodes locked
Cancelling the dialog leaves no new association and no ghost edge
Confirming the dialog creates the association, graph repaints, Pinia store updated
Clicking an edge in connect mode shows delete snackbar naming source and target
Confirming delete removes the association, graph repaints
Switching view mode while connect mode is active automatically exits connect mode
Existing side-panel add/remove flow is unaffected

ralph_loop.validate_command: npm run lint && npm run test
